{
  "file": {
    "content": "    pub(crate) fn scroll_into_view_with_options(\n        &self,\n        behavior: ScrollBehavior,\n        block: ScrollAxisState,\n        inline: ScrollAxisState,\n        container: Option<&Element>,\n        inner_target_rect: Option<Rect<Au, CSSPixel>>,\n    ) {\n        let get_target_rect = || match inner_target_rect {\n            None => self.upcast::<Node>().border_box().unwrap_or_default(),\n            Some(inner_target_rect) => inner_target_rect.translate(\n                self.upcast::<Node>()\n                    .content_box()\n                    .unwrap_or_default()\n                    .origin\n                    .to_vector(),\n            ),\n        };\n\n        // Step 1: For each ancestor element or viewport that establishes a scrolling box `scrolling\n        // box`, in order of innermost to outermost scrolling box, run these substeps:\n        let mut parent_scrolling_box = self.scrolling_box(ScrollContainerQueryFlags::empty());\n        while let Some(scrolling_box) = parent_scrolling_box {\n            parent_scrolling_box = scrolling_box.parent();\n\n            // Step 1.1: If the Document associated with `target` is not same origin with the\n            // Document associated with the element or viewport associated with `scrolling box`,\n            // terminate these steps.\n            //\n            // TODO: Handle this. We currently do not chain up to parent Documents.\n\n            // Step 1.2 Let `position` be the scroll position resulting from running the steps to\n            // determine the scroll-into-view position of `target` with `behavior` as the scroll\n            // behavior, `block` as the block flow position, `inline` as the inline base direction\n            // position and `scrolling box` as the scrolling box.\n            let position =\n                scrolling_box.determine_scroll_into_view_position(block, inline, get_target_rect());\n\n            // Step 1.3: If `position` is not the same as `scrolling box`\u2019s current scroll position, or\n            // `scrolling box` has an ongoing smooth scroll,\n            //\n            // TODO: Handle smooth scrolling.\n            if position != scrolling_box.scroll_position() {\n                //  \u21aa If `scrolling box` is associated with an element\n                //    Perform a scroll of the element\u2019s scrolling box to `position`,\n                //    with the `element` as the associated element and `behavior` as the\n                //    scroll behavior.\n                //  \u21aa If `scrolling box` is associated with a viewport\n                //    Step 1: Let `document` be the viewport\u2019s associated Document.\n                //    Step 2: Let `root element` be document\u2019s root element, if there is one, or\n                //    null otherwise.\n                //    Step 3: Perform a scroll of the viewport to `position`, with `root element`\n                //    as the associated element and `behavior` as the scroll behavior.\n                scrolling_box.scroll_to(position, behavior);\n            }\n\n            // Step 1.4: If `container` is not null and either `scrolling box` is a shadow-including\n            // inclusive ancestor of `container` or is a viewport whose document is a shadow-including\n            // inclusive ancestor of `container`, abort the rest of these steps.\n            if container.is_some_and(|container| {",
    "filePath": "/workspace/components/script/dom/element.rs",
    "numLines": 60,
    "startLine": 864,
    "totalLines": 5643

79. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/components/script/dom/scrolling_box.rs",
  "limit": 30,
  "offset": 161

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "    pub(crate) fn scroll_to(&self, position: LayoutVector2D, behavior: ScrollBehavior) {\n        match &self.target {\n            ScrollingBoxSource::Element(element) => {\n                element\n                    .owner_window()\n                    .scroll_an_element(element, position.x, position.y, behavior);\n            },\n            ScrollingBoxSource::Viewport(document) => {\n                document.window().scroll(position.x, position.y, behavior);\n            },\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn can_keyboard_scroll_in_axis(&self, axis: ScrollingBoxAxis) -> bool {\n        let overflow = match axis {\n            ScrollingBoxAxis::X => self.overflow.x,\n            ScrollingBoxAxis::Y => self.overflow.y,\n        };\n        if overflow == Overflow::Hidden {\n            return false;\n        }\n        match axis {\n            ScrollingBoxAxis::X => self.content_size().width > self.size().width,\n            ScrollingBoxAxis::Y => self.content_size().height > self.size().height,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#determine-the-scroll-into-view-position>\n    pub(crate) fn determine_scroll_into_view_position(\n        &self,",
    "filePath": "/workspace/components/script/dom/scrolling_box.rs",
    "numLines": 30,
    "startLine": 161,
    "totalLines": 307
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "stdout": "<!DOCTYPE HTML>\n<meta name=\"timeout\" content=\"long\">\n<meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width,initial-scale=1,minimum-scale=1\">\n<script src=\"/resources/testharness.js\"></script>\n<script src=\"/resources/testharnessreport.js\"></script>\n<script src=\"/resources/testdriver.js\"></script>\n<script src=\"/resources/testdriver-actions.js\"></script>\n<script src=\"/resources/testdriver-vendor.js\"></script>\n<script src=\"/common/subset-tests-by-key.js\"></script>\n<meta name=\"variant\" content=\"?include=subframe-inline-end-block-end-behavior-auto\"/>\n<meta name=\"variant\" content=\"?include=subframe-inline-start-block-start-behavior-smooth\"/>\n<meta name=\"variant\" content=\"?include=root-inline-end-block-end-behavior-auto\"/>\n<meta name=\"variant\" content=\"?include=root-inline-start-block-start-behavior-smooth\"/>\n<meta name=\"variant\" content=\"?include=nested-scrollIntoView\"/>\n<script src=\"scroll_support.js\"></script>\n<style>\nhtml {\n  height: 3000px;\n  width: 3000px;\n}\n#targetDiv {\n  width: 200px;\n  height: 200px;\n  overflow: scroll;\n}\n\n#innerDiv {\n  width: 400px;\n  height: 400px;\n}\n</style>\n\n<body style=\"margin:0\" onload=runTest()>\n<div id=\"targetDiv\">\n  <div id=\"innerDiv\">\n  </div>\n</div>\n</body>\n<script>\nlet element_scrollend_arrived = false;\nlet document_scrollend_arrived = false;\n\nfunction onElementScrollEnd(event) {\n  assert_false(event.cancelable);\n  assert_false(event.bubbles);\n  element_scrollend_arrived = true;\n}\n\nfunction onDocumentScrollEnd(event) {\n  assert_false(event.cancelable);\n  // scrollend events are bubbled when the target node is document.\n  assert_true(event.bubbles);\n  document_scrollend_arrived = true;\n}\n\nlet root_element = document.scrollingElement;\nlet target_div = document.getElementById(\"targetDiv\");\nlet inner_div = document.getElementById(\"innerDiv\");\n\n// Get expected position for root_element scrollIntoView.\nroot_element.scrollTo(10000, 10000);\nlet max_root_x = root_element.scrollLeft;\nlet max_root_y = root_element.scrollTop;\nroot_element.scrollTo(0, 0);\n\ntarget_div.scrollTo(10000, 10000);\nlet max_element_x = target_div.scrollLeft;\nlet max_element_y = target_div.scrollTop;\ntarget_div.scrollTo(0, 0);\n\nlet test_cases = [\n  {\n    key: \"subframe-inline-end-block-end-behavior-auto\",\n    args: { inline: \"end\", block: \"end\", behavior: \"smooth\" },\n    target: target_div,\n    scroll_target: inner_div,\n    expected: {\n      pos: { x: max_element_x, y: max_element_y },\n    },\n    title: \"Tests scrollend event for scrollIntoView with behavior 'auto' on subframe.\","
}

        // Step 4.

        self.pending_scroll_event_targets

Before
        // Step 4.

    /// Whenever a viewport gets scrolled (whether in response to user interaction or by an
    /// API), the user agent must run these steps:
    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#scrolling-events>
    pub(crate) fn handle_viewport_scroll_event(&self) {
After
        // Step 4.
        // > Run the steps to dispatch pending scrollsnapchange events for doc.
        // TODO(#7673): Implement scroll snapping
    }

    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#run-the-scrollend-steps>
    pub(crate) fn run_the_scrollend_steps(&self, can_gc: CanGc) {
        // Since Servo only performs instant (non-smooth) scrolls, any target in
        // pending_scrollend_event_targets has already finished scrolling by the
        // time this runs, so we unconditionally fire scrollend for each target.
        rooted_vec!(let notify_list <- self.pending_scrollend_event_targets.take().into_iter());
        for target in notify_list.iter() {
            if target.downcast::<Document>().is_some() {
                // Fire an event named scrollend that bubbles at target.
                target.fire_bubbling_event(atom!("scrollend"), can_gc);
            } else if target.downcast::<Element>().is_some() {
                // Fire an event named scrollend at target.
                target.fire_event(atom!("scrollend"), can_gc);
            }
        }
    }

    /// Whenever a viewport gets scrolled (whether in response to user interaction or by an
    /// API), the user agent must run these steps:
    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#scrolling-events>
    pub(crate) fn handle_viewport_scroll_event(&self) {
6. EDIT /workspace/components/script/dom/document.rs
Before
    /// Whether or not this [`Document`] has any pending scroll events to be processed during
    /// "update the rendering."
    fn has_pending_scroll_events(&self) -> bool {
        !self.pending_scroll_event_targets.borrow().is_empty()
    }
After
    /// Whether or not this [`Document`] has any pending scroll events to be processed during
    /// "update the rendering."
    fn has_pending_scroll_events(&self) -> bool {
        !self.pending_scroll_event_targets.borrow().is_empty()
    }

    /// Whether or not this [`Document`] has any pending scrollend events to be processed during
    /// "update the rendering."
    fn has_pending_scrollend_events(&self) -> bool {
        !self.pending_scrollend_event_targets.borrow().is_empty()
    }

After

Before
                target.fire_bubbling_event(atom!("scrollend"), can_gc);
            } else if target.downcast::<Element>().is_some() {
                // Fire an event named scrollend at target.
                target.fire_event(atom!("scrollend"), can_gc);
After
                target.fire_bubbling_event(Atom::from("scrollend"), can_gc);
            } else if target.downcast::<Element>().is_some() {
                // Fire an event named scrollend at target.
                target.fire_event(Atom::from("scrollend"), can_gc);
9. EDIT /workspace/components/script/script_thread.rs
Before
            // > 9. For each doc of docs, run the scroll steps for doc.
            document.run_the_scroll_steps(can_gc);

            // Media queries is only relevant when there are resizing.
After
            // > 9. For each doc of docs, run the scroll steps for doc.
            document.run_the_scroll_steps(can_gc);

            // > 9. (scrollend) For each doc of docs, run the scrollend steps for doc.
            document.run_the_scrollend_steps(can_gc);

            // Media queries is only relevant when there are resizing.
